# checks.license.LicenseCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.license.LicenseCheck }

```python
checks.license.LicenseCheck()
```

Check that the package has a license file.

Looks for common license file names (LICENSE, COPYING, etc.) and
attempts to identify the license type (MIT, Apache, GPL, etc.).

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("license").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

<code>[**LICENSE_FILENAMES**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of recognized license file names.

<code>[**KNOWN_LICENSES**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Dictionary mapping license identifiers to text markers used for identification.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run the license check:

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = LicenseCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.name
'license'
```

The check identifies common licenses:

```python
>>> # For a package with MIT license
>>> # result.status == CheckStatus.OK
>>> # "License type: MIT" in result.details
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.license.LicenseCheck.run) | Check for license file presence and validity. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.license.LicenseCheck.run }

```python
checks.license.LicenseCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Check for license file presence and validity.

Searches for a license file using common naming conventions,
verifies it's not empty, and attempts to identify the license type.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary for this check. Currently no check-specific options are used.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if a valid license file is found

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - WARNING status if no license file found or file is empty
